title: Card
description: A study card in a subject's `cards` module. Every PUBLISHED card must carry a primary-source citation (`source`) and a last-verified date (`verified`). Local `--draft` builds may omit those two while sourcing is in flight; the publish gate (`build.py --check`) requires them.

id.description: Stable, unique-within-subject slug. Used as the localStorage/state key — renaming it resets that card's progress, so it must never change once published.

tags.description: Optional lowercase keyword tags for filter/search.

level.description: Optional difficulty, by the COGNITIVE DEMAND of the answer (not topic obscurity): foundational (vocabulary / what-is recall) | intermediate (applied / how-to / day-to-day, the 'home turf' tier) | advanced (in-domain judgment, tradeoffs, 'what's wrong with this') | expert (cross-domain synthesis, novel application under uncertainty). Editorial judgment, NOT provenance-gated — an agent may propose it, a human approves it, no citation required.

a.description: Answer / back of the card. May contain newlines.

source.description: REQUIRED (for publish) authoritative primary-doc URL. Must be https://. Host is checked against the allowlist in config/gate.json.

verified.description: REQUIRED (for publish) ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the answer was last fact-checked against `source`. Set by a human, never by an agent.

unverified.description: Optional. When true, `source` is a confident citation that could NOT be independently fetch-checked (e.g. the host blocks automated access). The app shows an 'unverified' badge instead of a verified date, and `verified` then records when the citation was added rather than a fact-check date.

refs.description: Optional extra proof links shown in the detail drawer.
